subject: Fix drools-pdp to accommodate policy metadata values as objects
commit_message:
Fix drools-pdp to accommodate policy metadata values as objects

Issue-ID: POLICY-3832
Signed-off-by: zrrmmua <ramesh.murugan.iyer@est.tech>
Change-Id: I97b0962f76981941830f6712f3d824ca540951a2

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Both of them are test classes, wondering if you missed submitting the main code changes?

I see the order of the policy list is different in the expected and actual in testMergePolicies()  in LifeCycleFsmTest.java due to which the test case fails. Ideally the metadata should not have caused this.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Let's not merge these reviews until side effects of the changes are clear.
 
First question is to understand if there are any changes on how policies are serialized and sent down to the PDPs in the metadata section.    CSIT docker-compose should show that, we want to make sure that policy-id and policy-version still show as strings in the serialized policy sent by PAP.

Patch Set 3:

(3 comments)

In addition to this, we need to perform CSITs to make sure that the policy handling from API<---->PAP<----PDPs continues to work correctly.

Also, although this commit is building in gerrit, it is not building locally for either Ramesh or me, The test RestLifecycleManagerTest.testMultiPolicyFlow() when run on its own succeeds, when we run all the tests in feature-lifecycle, this test fails. It seems that somehow, the fsm.shutDown() is getting called before the test can finish.

Patch Set 3:

I have a similar comment in the models review.   I just would like to understand if there are any changes in the policy-id and policy-version for existing policies as serialized when sent from pap, introduced with the new functionality which is my concern for backwards compatibility reasons.   Do you have them handy, Ramesh, to post them?   I am looking for a PDP-UPDATE messages with policies to deploy from PAP for existing policies to check format, and for provisioning through policy API component.

Patch Set 3:

(2 comments)

I have another review Ramesh:  https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/policy/drools-pdp/+/127108
You can integrate those changes into yours, or drop this one and go with the other one.
In general, I am reluctant to any changes in the "domain" schemas for policy-id and policy-version, hence putting out the other review (and some changes to make set comparisons instead of list ones).
